thesis: Teradyne is the world's leading supplier of automated test equipment (ATE) for semiconductors and a direct beneficiary of the AI hardware buildout: AI now drives roughly 70% of its sales, and rising test intensity from advanced packaging, HBM/DRAM, and compute SoCs is structurally expanding its market. After a modest FY2025 (revenue +13% to $3.19B), demand has exploded — Q1 2026 revenue grew 87% and Q2 is guided to similar growth, with a $6B midterm revenue target at 59-61% gross margin. The problem is that this is still a cyclical business trading at a peak-cycle multiple: even after a ~34% pullback from its 52-week high, at ~$322 the stock trades near 60x trailing and ~46x forward earnings on what may be cyclically elevated earnings. Genuine AI tailwind, but rich valuation plus ATE's boom-bust history argue for patience — hence HOLD, with meaningful upside to the ~$423 analyst consensus if the up-cycle proves durable.

drivers: The AI-driven test super-cycle proves structural rather than a spike, with advanced packaging, HBM/DRAM, and compute SoC test intensity durably expanding Teradyne's served market toward a ~$20B ATE TAM
FY2026 revenue lands at or above guidance and Teradyne advances toward its $6B midterm revenue target at 59-61% gross margin
Share gains in compute, DRAM, and networking test hold, aided by the Tokyo Electron KGD collaboration for AI/data-center device screening
Robotics (Universal Robots, MiR) inflects on 'physical AI' adoption, adding a second growth engine and improving segment profitability
Strong free cash flow funds buybacks and the premium multiple holds as earnings compound

base_case.assumptions: Teradyne delivers a strong FY2026 (revenue well above the $3.19B FY2025 base on AI-led Semiconductor Test demand) with gross margin in the ~59-61% range, then growth moderates in 2027 as the initial AI test build digests. The multiple compresses modestly from ~46x forward as earnings are delivered rather than merely anticipated, leaving the stock drifting toward the ~$423 analyst consensus over the next year.

risks: The ATE cycle rolls over as AI test demand digests, and cyclically elevated earnings normalize sharply — Teradyne's historical boom-bust pattern reasserts itself
The premium ~46x forward / ~60x trailing multiple compresses toward the historical 20-25x range on any growth wobble
Customer concentration in a handful of large chipmakers and OSATs makes revenue lumpy and order timing volatile
Robotics remains subscale and a margin drag rather than the hoped-for second engine
A broad semiconductor-equipment or AI de-rating hits a high-beta (~1.7) name that has already run ~250% off its lows

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — AI test demand, order trends, and 2H 2026 outlook (consensus ~$2.04 EPS on ~$1.22B revenue)
Durability of the AI-led up-cycle and progress toward the $6B midterm revenue target at 59-61% gross margin
Ramp of the Tokyo Electron KGD (known-good-device) integrated test solution for AI and data-center devices
Robotics 'physical AI' adoption (Universal Robots, MiR) and defense/other test wins (e.g., $139.8M Air Force contract)
Q4/FY2026 results and initial FY2027 outlook — the key test of whether AI demand is structural or cyclical

key_risks: Cyclicality: automated test equipment is historically boom-bust; current earnings may be cyclically elevated near a peak
Full valuation: ~60x trailing and ~46x forward earnings, well above the historical 20-25x range, embed continued strong execution
Customer concentration among a few large chipmakers and OSATs makes orders lumpy and revenue volatile
Dependence on the AI capex cycle: AI is ~70% of sales, so an AI-spending digestion phase hits Teradyne disproportionately
Robotics segment remains subscale and can weigh on blended margins
High beta (~1.7) and a ~250% run off the lows leave the stock exposed to a semi-equipment or AI de-rating; recent insider selling noted
